title: The Effects of Generative AI on Productivity, Innovation and Entrepreneurship
claim_supported: GenAI can lower innovation frictions and automate or augment tasks, but benefits depend on responsible adoption and complementary capabilities.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Competitive Dynamics in Downstream Markets
claim_supported: AI changes competitive dynamics downstream from the foundation-model layer, so app-level entry and concentration must be evaluated separately.

title: Market Power in Artificial Intelligence
claim_supported: AI market power can arise from training data, input data, predictions, compute, and distribution rather than only product features.

title: AI-Native Firms
claim_supported: AI-native startups can be smaller and more technically composed, supporting the view that AI changes organization shape without removing expertise requirements.

title: Generative AI Fuels Solo Entrepreneurship, but Teams Still Lead at the Top
claim_supported: GenAI can increase solo entrepreneurial entry while top-ranked outcomes still favor teams.

title: GenAI in Entrepreneurship
claim_supported: The entrepreneurship literature is expanding but still needs more macro-level and regulatory research on GenAI as an external enabler.

title: Generative AI Use in Entrepreneurship
claim_supported: GenAI can empower entrepreneurs while also causing overconfidence, workslop, hallucination, and reduced learning.

title: AI as Co-founder
claim_supported: The ChatGPT shock is associated with more small-firm entry in areas with stronger AI human capital.

title: Large Language Models for Software Engineering
claim_supported: LLM4SE research is broad and rapidly growing, but evaluation, limitations, and lifecycle coverage remain unsettled.

title: Faster Code, Deeper Debt?
claim_supported: LLM-assisted development can amplify code, design, documentation, prompt, provenance, and governance debt.

title: TAM-Eval
claim_supported: LLMs have limited capability in realistic unit-test creation, repair, and update scenarios.

title: The Current Challenges of Software Engineering in the Era of Large Language Models
claim_supported: LLM-based software engineering faces challenges across requirements, design, coding, testing, review, maintenance, security, data, training, and evaluation.

title: AI-Assisted Programming Decreases the Productivity of Experienced Developers by Increasing the Technical Debt and Maintenance Burden
claim_supported: AI-assisted productivity gains may shift rework and review burden toward experienced maintainers.

title: Promptware Engineering
claim_supported: Prompt-enabled systems need software engineering discipline across requirements, testing, debugging, evolution, deployment, and monitoring.

title: PromptDebt
claim_supported: Prompt design, hyperparameter tuning, and LLM-framework integration create LLM-specific self-admitted technical debt.

title: Investigating and Designing for Trust in AI-powered Code Generation Tools
claim_supported: Developer trust in AI code generation is situational and depends on expectation-setting, configurability, and validation affordances.

title: Mapping the Trust Terrain
claim_supported: Trust-related concepts for LLMs in software engineering require clearer definitions and better calibration.

title: How Developers Interact with AI
claim_supported: Developer-AI workflows include multiple interaction modes and require more research on control, trust, and usability.

title: The New Compiler Stack
claim_supported: LLM-enabled compilation offers new opportunities but correctness and scalability remain core challenges.

title: Agentic AI Software Engineers
claim_supported: Future agentic software engineering should shift from programming at scale to programming with trust.

title: Reproducible, Explainable, and Effective Evaluations of Agentic AI for Software Engineering
claim_supported: Agentic AI evaluations should expose trajectories and interaction data to support reproducible comparison.
